description: Analyze the spatial concentration of multi-worker households in San Francisco using UrbanSim microdata. Using the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/worker_demographics_analysis.ipynb and data from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, categorize households by worker count (Zero-Worker: 0, Single-Worker: 1, Multi-Worker: >=2). Calculate citywide average income and total household counts for these categories, saving the results to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/citywide_worker_income.json with keys 'avg_income_zero_worker', 'avg_income_single_worker', 'avg_income_multi_worker', and 'citywide_total_households'. Aggregate this data to the zone level by joining households, buildings, and parcels. For zones with at least 50 households, calculate total_households, zero_worker_count, single_worker_count, multi_worker_count, pct_multi_worker, and avg_household_income. Export this filtered data to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_worker_profiles.csv. Finally, generate a scatter plot of pct_multi_worker (X) vs avg_household_income (Y) and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/worker_income_scatter.png. Ensure all notebook cells are executed.
